rag_rules: Insert generated annex content into the owning component or implementation scope that matches the content kind, while preserving existing architecture elements unchanged.
Preserve structural context: do not alter component declarations, features, subcomponents, connections, or property associations when adding annex material.
Keep references grounded in names visible from the chosen owner scope; avoid introducing content that depends on out-of-scope identifiers.
generic_rules: Use component type scope for interface-level contract content and implementation scope for implementation-level bindings only when that scope is required.
Maintain owner/scope consistency so inserted content does not reference subcomponent internals from a broader scope.
Apply minimal edits: add the annex block without restructuring unrelated AADL text.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level additions belong under the implementation owner after relevant structural declarations when that scope is needed.
anti_patterns: Do not change architecture structure or move existing declarations just to accommodate annex insertion.
Do not insert references to AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were directly visible variables.
Do not use identifiers that are not grounded in the current model or explicitly declared in the inserted content.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement semantics about when an output must hold under a flight-condition trigger
Concrete conditional-expression contract examples for deriving output behavior
